title: Child Development and Learning
catalogDescription: Uses readings, discussions, naturalistic observations, and "virtual" children to understand the different ways children develop - physically, cognitively, social-emotionally, in language and literacy, and in approaches to learning - during early childhood, the interplay between each of these domains and the environmental context, and the theories about the developmental mechanisms underlying these changes.

title: Adolescent Development
catalogDescription: Students use readings, discussion, and interview data to understand adolescent development. Discusses multiple domains of development, as well as the contextually situated nature of adolescence. Highlights implications for educators, policy makers, and others who serve the needs of adolescents throughout the course. Course overlaps with: B EDUC 481.

title: Applied Child Development and Learning
catalogDescription: Examination of the latest research on brain development in early childhood and the application of this knowledge in the creation of learning environments that promote physical, language, cognitive, social, and emotional development. The roles of family, classroom, and community provide context to build relationships that support each child's development.

title: Adult Learning and Development
catalogDescription: Examination of current research and practice in the field of adult education, learning, and development.

title: Learning and Teaching in our Changing World: Insights from Experience and Research
catalogDescription: Human learning in the educational setting. Cognition, development, learning, motivation, affective processes, and socialization. Emphasis on skills in influencing classroom learning and discipline.

effectiveYearRanges.note: Parsed from the official UW Seattle course descriptions page.

sourceLinks.label: UW Seattle course descriptions (EDPSY)
sourceLinks.note: Course detail parsed from the official UW course descriptions page.

notes: UW Seattle catalog metadata parsed from the official UW course descriptions.

title: Introduction to Educational Measurement and Analytics
catalogDescription: Introduces core concepts in measurement scales, sampling distributions (frequentist perspective), null hypothesis significance testing, confidence intervals, and power for 1- and 2-group z/t/chi/F tests, simple linear correlation and regression, and scale reliability and validity (foundational classic test theory). Covers basic coding skills for data analysis and visualization. Recommended: coursework in either pre-algebra or algebra.

title: Human Learning and Educational Practice
catalogDescription: Systematic examination of current research about human learning in educational settings, including the study of behavioral, information processing, social construction, and the developmental perspectives on learning.

title: Developmental Foundations of Early Learning
catalogDescription: Perceptual-motor, language, and overall cognitive development in children from birth through primary-school age. Emphasis on Piagetian and Vygotskian approaches to development with a special focus on the connections between learning and development. Field-based course projects may be required. Prerequisite: EDPSY 501.

title: Culture Learning and Development
catalogDescription: Explores influential contemporary research and theory that view culture as foundational to learning and development. Considers these issues in a range of settings (classrooms, informal environments, families, early childhood to adult learners, etc.) and within specific domains (e.g. science, literacy, mathematics). Prerequisite: EDPSY 501. Credit/no-credit only.

title: Race Theories and Education
catalogDescription: Reviews major race frameworks used in educational research such as critical race theory, Afropessimism, cultural studies perspectives, Whiteness studies, etc. Explores the intellectual history and core concepts of these frameworks, by reading both foundational, primary source academic literature and speculative fiction that applies ideas from the frameworks.

title: Applied Measurement in Educational Research
catalogDescription: Examines typical quantitative research designs in educational research with specific regard to different kinds of validity considerations, including critical quantitative methods. Project focuses on proposal for developing, adapting, refining, or re-purposing an observed measure of a latent construct. Teaches the American Psychological Association (APA) citation style. Prerequisite: EDPSY 490.

title: Adolescence and Youth
catalogDescription: Developmental processes and patterns examined with major theoretical and current research themes from behavioral sciences as applied to middle school and senior high students. Educational issues, social problems associated with adolescence in Western culture. Prerequisite: EDPSY 501.

title: Multiple Regression
catalogDescription: Quantitative methods for students in the social, behavioral, and health sciences. Focuses on understanding, estimating, interpreting, and reporting results for univariate multiple regression models for metrical and categorical outcomes, from a frequentist perspective. Prerequisite: EDPSY 490.

title: Classic Test Theory Reliability and Validity
catalogDescription: An examination of assumptions involved in Classic Test Theory, including measurement error, factors affecting reliability and validity, item analysis, and standards for educational and psychological measurement. Prerequisite: EDPSY 538.

title: Generalizability Theory
catalogDescription: Focuses on various designs and applications used with the Generalizability theory framework to estimate the amount of score variation associated with a measurement procedure, approach the reliability and validity issues, and optimize the measurement procedure. Prerequisite: EDPSY 539; recommended: introduction to educational statistics and measurement so that students are familiar with the basic concepts and procedures that we will continue working on in this course.

title: Structural Equation Modeling I
catalogDescription: Theory and data analysis using linear structural equation models. Topics include path analysis, exploratory and confirmatory latent variable factor analysis, and structural regression models to evaluate relationships among latent variables. Prerequisite: EDPSY 538.

title: Multilevel Modeling
catalogDescription: Theory and data analysis for modeling nested data with random factors, including linear and generalized linear hierarchical models for cross-sectional data as well as basic growth models. Prerequisite: EDPSY 538.

catalogDescription: Advanced seminar on selected topics in educational psychology. A critical appraisal of current research.

title: Item Response Theory I
catalogDescription: Explores assumptions and applications of Item Response Theory (IRT) measurement models, testing and evaluating differential item functioning, and procedures for equating and linking. Prerequisite: EDPSY 539. Offered: jointly with CSSS 591.
